title: SwarmKit ExecutorAdapter
description: A declarative harness adapter (design/details/executor-declarative-adapters-plan.md). Defines how to launch an external agentic harness as a subprocess and map its line-delimited JSON output into the normalized ExecEvent vocabulary — so a new harness is added as data (this artifact), with no Python and no runtime release. The DSL is deliberately minimal (RFC executor-abstraction.md decision 1a): JSONL streams only, literal-equality matching, dotted-path/for_each extraction, a named map for enum translation; anything past that ceiling declares `requires: code` and graduates to a Tier-1 Python Executor.

identifier.description: lowercase-kebab id; this is the executor `kind` an archetype selects.

template.description: A value template. May contain closed substitution variables in braces — {task.statement}, {task.base_ref}, {sandbox.root}, {budget.max_turns}, {budget.max_cost_usd}, {budget.max_wall_clock_minutes}, {resume.token}, {config.*}, {credential.model_provider}. Substituted into argv positions only — never concatenated into a shell string.

extract_or_literal.description: A value in an event `with`/`set` block. A string starting with `$.` is a dotted-path extraction from the parsed JSON line (with array indexing; iterate arrays via a rule's `for_each`); any other scalar is a literal.

status_map.description: Vendor discriminator value -> ExecResultStatus (success|failure|budget_exceeded|cancelled|needs_approval|stalled). `_default` covers the rest.

success_when.description: Terminal success predicate. Core layers its semantic check (typed output + artifact-manifest match) on top — exit code alone is necessary, not sufficient.

on_unanswerable.description: Interaction ceiling for a declarative adapter (RFC §5.2): deny refuses the out-of-grant action in place; abort terminates needs_approval. `relay` is Tier-1-only (needs bidirectional session control) and is not permitted here.

telemetry_grade.description: `opaque` (unobservable) adapters are denied by default (RFC decision 5); use requires explicit per-archetype opt-in.

requires.description: Present only when this adapter has hit the declarative DSL ceiling and must be implemented as a Tier-1 Python Executor instead. A validator/loader treats `requires: code` as 'not runnable declaratively'.

launch.description: How to launch the harness subprocess. `command` is argv (no shell); substitution is value-only.

optional_args.description: Arg groups appended only when the referenced variable is set; the whole group drops when it is empty.

when.description: A substitution variable name (without braces), e.g. `budget.max_turns` or `config.model`.

env.description: Environment variables injected at launch. The only allowed secret is the model-provider credential (RFC §7): {credential.model_provider}. All other secrets are proxy-injected only (not here).

auth.description: Which authentication modes the harness supports (RFC decision 4), expressed generically. A mode contributes to the launch however the harness needs — env vars, extra command args, and/or provisioned credential paths — so auth may be an env var, a command flag, saved CLI credentials, or any combination; the engine has no per-mode special-casing. Both modes may be declared; `default` sets the mode used when the workspace/archetype does not override. In headless mode api_key takes precedence over subscription where both are usable.

default.description: The auth mode used when the workspace/archetype does not override.

modes.description: The auth modes this adapter can run under, keyed by mode name; each value declares that mode's contribution to the launch.

auth_mode.description: What an auth mode contributes to the launch. All parts optional — combine as the harness requires. Env and args are merged into the launch command/environment when this mode is active; credential_paths are provisioned into the sandbox.

env.description: Env vars this mode injects, e.g. {"ANTHROPIC_API_KEY": "{credential.model_provider}"}.

args.description: Command args this mode appends, e.g. [--api-key, "{credential.model_provider}"] — for harnesses that take the key on the command line rather than the environment.

credential_paths.description: Paths (files/dirs) holding the vendor's saved auth state to provision into the sandbox, e.g. ~/.claude — for subscription/CLI-login modes.

retain_raw.description: Tee each untranslated vendor line as exec.raw for a forensic trail.

rule.description: One event-map rule: match a parsed JSON line, optionally iterate an array, capture state, and/or emit ExecEvents.

when.description: Literal-equality match on dotted field paths of the parsed line (keys are dotted paths, values are the required literals). Empty/absent = matches every line.

for_each.description: A dotted path to an array; the rule's `emit` runs once per item, with `$.` resolving against the item.

set.description: Capture values into adapter state (no event). The only recognized key is `session_id` (used for resume tokens).

emit_spec.description: Emit one ExecEvent. `with` maps event fields to extracted values or literals.

when.description: Optional per-item literal match (used inside a `for_each` to select array items by type).

with.description: Event field -> extraction path ($.a.b) or literal. A field whose value is {"from": "$.x", "map": "status_map"} is translated through the named map (the only enum-translation primitive).

resume.description: Makes resume-token support declarative: the captured session_id is replayed into the launch command on a retry/resume.
